algorithms.title: Suspected Diabetic Emergency Field Approach
algorithms.description: Sequence for evaluating altered mental status, vomiting, abdominal pain, or unexplained rapid breathing in a known or possible diabetic patient.
steps: Check blood glucose immediately in any altered, vomiting, or unusually tachypneic patient with known or suspected diabetes.
If glucose is low: treat hypoglycemia per protocol (oral glucose if awake/cooperative; IV dextrose or IM glucagon per protocol if not) and reassess.
If glucose is high or only mildly elevated with Kussmaul respirations, fruity breath odor, vomiting/abdominal pain, and dehydration: suspect DKA, including euglycemic DKA if glucose is near-normal.
If glucose is very high with profound dehydration, slower onset, minimal respiratory compensation, and prominent altered mental status/obtundation: suspect HHS.
For suspected DKA or HHS: provide supportive care (oxygen as needed, IV access and isotonic fluids per protocol, cardiac monitoring), protect the airway given vomiting risk.
Transport rapidly for definitive hospital treatment (insulin, careful fluid/electrolyte correction) — do not attempt to correct DKA/HHS glucose with field insulin.
